user_query:
Our OpenClaw gateway restarted and we need a safe, deterministic audit of orphaned browser processes. Read input/processes.csv and input/policy.yaml, then:

1) Identify OpenClaw-launched browser processes for the target user and classify which are zombies using these rules:
- Only include processes where owner matches the user in input/policy.yaml
- Must be a browser process with executable name containing one of: chrome, chromium, brave, msedge, firefox (case-sensitive substring in the cmdline is fine)
- Must have the OpenClaw user-data-dir pattern from input/policy.yaml present in the cmdline (e.g., ".openclaw/browser/")
- Orphaned if ppid == 1 OR parent_cmdline contains the substring "systemd --user"
- Must be at least min_age seconds old (>= min_age from policy)

2) Produce two files under output/:
- output/report.json with the exact JSON structure:
  {
    "total_openclaw_browsers": <int>,
    "zombie_count": <int>,
    "zombie_memory_mb": <int>,
    "zombies": [{"pid": <int>, "ppid": <int>, "age_seconds": <int>, "rss_kb": <int>, "cmdline": "<string>"}]
  }
  Where:
  - total_openclaw_browsers: count of processes that match owner + browser + pattern (regardless of orphan/age)
  - zombie_count: number of entries in zombies[]
  - zombie_memory_mb: sum of rss_kb for zombies converted to MB using integer division (floor), i.e., floor(sum(rss_kb) / 1024)
  - zombies: include only the processes that satisfy ALL zombie rules; each entry must include the listed fields
- output/kill_list.txt containing only the zombie PIDs, one per line, sorted ascending. (We are in detect-only mode; this is a recommended kill list, do not perform any actions.)

Notes:
- input/processes.csv has headers: pid,ppid,owner,age_seconds,rss_kb,cmdline,parent_cmdline
- input/policy.yaml includes: user (string), min_age (int), pattern (string), mode (string; e.g., "detect"). Use these values as your parameters. If mode != "kill", still produce kill_list.txt as the recommended list.
- Treat equality on age_seconds as inclusive (>= min_age qualifies).
